docs.title: How it works
docs.text: BurnZone is one backend and a shared core, with two thin apps on top. That's what lets a member app and an operator console stay consistent, secure and cheap to evolve.

docs.title: The architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
        │   Member app (Flutter)      │     │   Operator console (Flutter)│
        │   iOS · Android · Web       │     │   Web-first                 │
        │   Train · Eat · Book · Track│     │   Dashboard · Branches ·    │
        │                             │     │   Bookings · Users · Plans  │
        └──────────────┬──────────────┘     └──────────────┬──────────────┘
                       │                                    │
                       └───────────────┬────────────────────┘
                                       │   shared Dart core
                                       │   (API client · models · auth/session
                                       │    · theme · role & scope gating)
                                       ▼
                        ┌───────────────────────────────────┐
                        │   Backend API (Express + TS)      │
                        │   JWT auth · RBAC · multi-tenant  │
                        │   ~200 endpoints                  │
                        └───────────────┬───────────────────┘
                                        ▼
                        ┌───────────────────────────────────┐
                        │   PostgreSQL (tenant-scoped)      │
                        └───────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: The pieces
docs.text: Component What it is Member app A Flutter app (one codebase → iOS, Android, web) for members. Operator console A Flutter web app for staff, with role- and branch-gated sections. Shared core A Dart package both apps depend on: typed API client, data models, session/auth, the design system, and the role/scope gating logic — so the apps stay thin and identical in behaviour. Backend An Express + TypeScript API over PostgreSQL: authentication, RBAC, multi-tenancy, bookings, nutrition, workouts, SaaS plans.

docs.title: How a request stays safe
docs.text:
1. A user signs in → the backend issues a short-lived access token and a refresh token (JWT).
  2. The app stores them and silently refreshes the access token when it expires — so sessions don't drop mid-use, and they survive reloads.
  3. Every API call carries the token; the backend re-derives the caller's tenant, role and branch scope and authorises each route. The UI gating is a convenience, never the only check.

docs.title: One experience, every channel
docs.text: Because the member and admin apps are Flutter over a shared Dart core, the same logic ships to iOS, Android and the web. Branding is applied at runtime per tenant, so one build becomes any gym's app. Fixing a bug or adding a model in the core improves both apps at once.

docs.title: Built to operate
docs.text:
• Multi-tenant data isolation keyed on tenant_id
  • RBAC on every route; branch scope narrows it further
  • Audit logging of sensitive actions
  • CORS allow-listing, security headers, and JWT refresh
  • Deployable to managed cloud (the API runs on Cloud Run + Cloud SQL)

See exactly what's shipped on What's available →.

docs.title: What's available
docs.text:
An honest, current snapshot of what BurnZone does today. We'd rather under-promise here than oversell.

Live usable in the product today   ·   Beta backend/API built; dedicated screen rolling out   ·   Planned on the roadmap

How we read these badges

A capability is only Live if you can actually use it in an app today. Where the backend fully supports something but its dedicated screen is still being built, we mark it Beta — not Live — even though the platform can already do it.

docs.title: Platform & accounts
docs.text:
Capability Status Multi-tenant backend with per-tenant data isolation Live White-label branding (name, colours, logo, display name) Live Email/password auth, JWT with silent refresh, persisted sessions Live Role-based access control (Super Admin / Branch Admin / Trainer / Member) Live Branch scope enforced on the backend Live Audit logging of sensitive actions Live Email notifications on booking confirm / cancel Live Social / OAuth sign-in (Google, Facebook, Instagram) Planned Custom domains per tenant Beta

Social sign-in has scaffolded backend endpoints but is not yet integrated with live providers, and there's no in-app button yet — so we call it Planned.

docs.title: Member app
docs.text:
Capability Status Sign in; stay signed in across reloads & restarts Live Home dashboard (calories vs target, streak, goal progress) Live Workout library Live Curated programs Live Log meals; daily calorie & macro summary Live View your goal & its automatic daily calorie target Live Record weigh-ins; see your latest weight Live Consistency streaks Live Book & cancel trainer sessions Live Create / edit a goal in-app Beta Personal workout playlists (with review) Beta AI photo calorie estimation Planned Wearable / health sync Planned Push notifications Planned

Goals, playlists and the booking lifecycle are fully built in the API; the member app reads goals today and the dedicated create/edit and playlist screens are the next surfaces to land — hence Beta.

docs.title: Operator console
docs.text:
The console gives operators live operational visibility across the gym today, plus branch creation. Deeper write actions are supported by the API and are landing in the console next.

Capability Status Dashboard with live KPIs + recent bookings Live Branches — list & create Live Users — live directory (roles & status) Live Bookings — live overview with colour-coded statuses Live Plans — view tiers, limits & feature flags Live Tenants — view provisioned gyms (Super Admin) Live Booking actions in console (complete / cancel / no-show / override) Beta User actions in console (invite, change role / status) Beta Branch ↔ user / trainer assignment in console Beta Plan & tenant editing in console (provision / suspend) Beta Trainer availability & sessions screens Beta Branch calendar view Beta Plan-limit enforcement at creation time Beta Compliance / privacy-request console Planned Billing & payment collection Planned

Every Beta row above is already supported by the backend API (e.g. completing a booking, inviting a user, provisioning a tenant) — what's rolling out is the console screen that drives it.

docs.title: Quality & delivery
docs.text:
Status Backend test suites (unit · BDD · white-box · black-box · data-driven) Live App widget/smoke tests for member & admin screens Live Production web builds for both apps Live API deployed on managed cloud (Cloud Run + Cloud SQL) Live iOS / Android store packaging Planned

Questions about a specific capability? The For gyms and For members sections go a level deeper.

docs.title: BurnZone for gyms
docs.text:
Launch a branded member app and a staff console — without building software.

BurnZone is a multi-tenant, white-label gym platform. You get a polished member experience and an operator console that share one secure backend, branded to your gym and segregated by role and branch.

Live means it works today. Beta is wired but maturing. Planned is on the roadmap.

docs.title: Your brand, not ours
docs.text: Every gym is a tenant with its own name, colours and logo. Members install your app; staff sign into your console. One build serves all tenants.

docs.title: Multiple branches
docs.text: Model your real estate. Assign trainers and members to branches; scope every view, booking and report to the right location.

docs.title: Roles that match your org
docs.text: Super Admin, Branch Admin and Trainer live in one console, each seeing only what their role and branch allow.

docs.title: What you can do today
docs.text:
Capability Status Branded tenants (name, colours, logo, display name) Live Operator dashboard with live KPIs Live Branches — list & create Live Live directories — users, bookings, plans, tenants Live Trainer availability → bookable slots → bookings (full lifecycle) Live Workout library & curated programs Live Tiered SaaS plans (Free / Premium / Pro) with limits & feature flags Live Management actions in console (invite, roles, assignments, booking actions, provisioning) Beta Per-tenant custom domain Beta Plan-limit enforcement at creation Beta Billing & payment collection Planned

Read views vs. write actions

The console gives operators live read visibility across the gym today (dashboards + directories) plus branch creation. The deeper management actions are built in the API and are rolling into the console — they're marked Beta, not Live, until their screen ships.

See the full, honest breakdown on What's available.

docs.title: Explore the platform
docs.text:
• White-label & multi-tenant — how branding and isolation work
  • Operator console — dashboard, branches, bookings, users, plans
  • Roles & access — who can do what, and how scope is enforced
  • Plans & pricing — the tiers and their limits

docs.title: Operator console
docs.text:
The console is a single web app (Flutter, web-first) where Super Admins, Branch Admins and Trainers run the gym. Each role sees a different set of sections — the navigation itself is gated.

Today the console delivers live operational visibility plus branch creation. The remaining management actions are already built in the API and are landing in the console as dedicated screens — marked Beta below.

docs.title: Dashboard Live
docs.text: A KPI overview computed live from the API: members, trainers, branches, bookings, plans — and tenants for Super Admins — plus a recent-bookings feed with colour-coded statuses.

docs.title: Branches Live
docs.text: List and create branches (name, address); every booking and schedule is branch-scoped, and Branch Admins are limited to the branches they manage. Assigning trainers and members to a branch is supported by the API and surfacing in the console next Beta.

docs.title: Bookings Live
docs.text: A live overview of the booking pipeline across the gym: who booked which trainer, at which branch, and when — with colour-coded statuses (confirmed, completed, cancelled, no_show). The actions behind those statuses — complete, cancel, no-show, override to a new slot — are live in the API and being wired into the console Beta.

docs.title: Users Live
docs.text: A live directory of everyone in the tenant with their role and status. Inviting new staff/members and changing roles or account status are API-backed and rolling into the console Beta.

docs.title: Plans Live
docs.text: View the SaaS tiers (Free / Premium / Pro), their per-tenant limits — branches, trainers, members — and feature flags. In-console editing of plans is next Beta. See Plans & pricing.

docs.title: Tenants Live
docs.text: Super-Admin-only: a live list of provisioned gyms with status. Provisioning new gyms, setting branding, and suspending/activating tenants are API-backed and surfacing in the console Beta.

docs.title: Trainer tools Beta
docs.text: Trainers get their own sections — My availability (set recurring hours and blocked dates, which generate bookable slots) and My sessions (upcoming & completed). The booking engine behind these is live; the trainer-facing screens are maturing.

docs.title: Compliance Planned
docs.text:
Privacy requests, consent management and audit review surfaces are planned on top of the audit logging the backend already records.

Next: Roles & access →

docs.title: Plans & pricing
docs.text:
BurnZone ships with tiered SaaS plans. Each plan sets limits (branches, trainers, members) and toggles features, enforced per tenant by the backend.

Prices below are the demo configuration — fully editable by a Super Admin in the console. Plans, limits and feature flags are data, not code.

docs.title: Free
docs.text:
$0 / month A starter tier for a single location.

  • 1 branch
  • Up to 3 trainers
  • Up to 50 members
  • Core member app + console

docs.title: Premium
docs.text:
$29.99 / month · $299/yr For growing multi-branch gyms.

  • Up to 3 branches
  • Up to 15 trainers
  • Up to 500 members
  • Programs, bookings, dashboards

docs.title: Pro
docs.text:
$59.99 / month · $599/yr For chains and franchises.

  • Up to 25 branches
  • Up to 100 trainers
  • Up to 5,000 members
  • Everything in Premium + custom domain Beta

docs.title: How limits work
docs.text:
Each plan defines its limits (branches, trainers, members) and feature flags, and the backend computes usage against them — so a Super Admin can see, per tenant, how close it is to each ceiling.

Hard enforcement at creation — blocking the 4th branch on a 3-branch plan, for example — is being rolled out Beta; today the limits are tracked and visible rather than blocking. The plan model is already shaped for enforcement, so turning it on is a contained change.

docs.title: Billing Planned
docs.text:
Plan definitions, limits and per-tenant assignment are live today. Payment collection (card capture, invoicing, dunning) is on the roadmap — the plan model is already shaped to plug a payment provider in.

Back to For gyms overview · or see What's available.

docs.title: Roles & access
docs.text: BurnZone segregates features by identity, role, scope and consent — so the same console and the same API serve very different people safely.

docs.title: The four roles
docs.text: Role Lives in Sees / can do Super Admin Console Everything in the tenant: tenants, plans, branches, bookings, users, dashboard Branch Admin Console Their branch(es): dashboard, branches, bookings, users — not platform-wide settings Trainer Console Their own availability & sessions Member Member app Train, eat, book, track — only their own data

docs.title: How access is enforced
docs.text:
Access is checked in two places, so the UI and the data agree:

  1. In the apps — navigation and actions are gated by role. A Branch Admin never sees the Tenants or Plans sections; a Trainer only sees their own tools.
  2. On the backend — every route re-checks the caller's role and tenant and branch scope. Hiding a button is never the only defence.

Scope in practice

A Branch Admin requesting trainers for a branch they don't manage is rejected by the API (403), even if they craft the request by hand. Members can list bookable trainers at their own branch, but not the admin-only staff roster.

docs.title: Consent-aware features
docs.text: Sensitive or AI-assisted features can require an explicit consent grant in addition to a plan flag — the access layer combines flag-enabled and consented before a feature is usable. This keeps privacy-sensitive capabilities opt-in.

docs.title: Why it matters for operators
docs.text:
• Delegate safely — give branch managers real power, bounded to their branch.
  • Least privilege — trainers and members can't reach operator surfaces.
  • One console — no separate apps to maintain per role; the experience adapts.

Next: Plans & pricing →

docs.title: White-label & multi-tenant
docs.text: BurnZone is multi-tenant: a single backend and a single app build serve every gym. Each gym is a tenant with isolated data and its own branding.

docs.title: One build, many brands
docs.text:
The apps don't hard-code a gym. The API base URL and tenant are supplied at runtime, and branding is fetched from the backend — so the same binary becomes your app:

  • App name & display name — what members see in the app and on their home screen
  • Brand colours — a primary/secondary palette themes the whole UI
  • Logo — shown in-app and on the console

How a member's app knows which gym it is

The tenant is resolved at runtime (a gym code / subdomain or a build-time define). The app then loads that tenant's branding and shows only that gym's content. No rebuild per gym.

docs.title: Data isolation
docs.text:
Every record — users, branches, workouts, bookings, nutrition logs — is keyed to a tenant_id. Queries are tenant-scoped end-to-end, so one gym can never see another's data. Roles and branch scope narrow access further within a tenant.

Tenant: "BurnZone Demo Gym"          Tenant: "Iron Republic"
 ├─ Branches: Downtown, Riverside      ├─ Branches: …
 ├─ Staff: admins, trainers            ├─ Staff: …
 ├─ Members                            ├─ Members
 └─ Workouts, bookings, nutrition      └─ … (fully separate)

docs.title: Custom domains Beta
docs.text: Tenants can register a custom domain (e.g. app.yourgym.com) so the member web app and console live under your own URL. Domain records and verification are modelled in the backend; automated certificate provisioning is being finalised.

docs.title: Settings per tenant
docs.text:
Each tenant carries operational defaults that the apps respect:

  • Booking duration & cancellation policy window
  • Timezone, currency and language
  • Notification preferences

Next: Operator console →

docs.title: BurnZone for members
docs.text:
Everything for your fitness journey — in one app.

BurnZone's member app brings your workouts, nutrition, trainer bookings and progress together. It's a real, native-feeling app on iOS, Android and the web — fast, clean, and built around staying consistent.

docs.title: Home
docs.text: Your daily snapshot: calories vs target, your streak and goal progress — with quick actions to log a meal, browse workouts or book a session.

docs.title: Train
docs.text: A library of workouts and curated programs — duration, difficulty, estimated burn and a video link to follow along.

docs.title: Eat
docs.text: Log meals in seconds; see today's total against your daily target and your protein / carbs / fat breakdown.

docs.title: Book
docs.text: Browse a trainer's available slots at your branch and book a 1:1 session — manage and cancel from one place.

docs.title: Track
docs.text: Set a goal, record weigh-ins, and watch your streak grow. Small, visible wins that keep you going.

docs.title: What works today
docs.text: Feature Status Sign in; stay signed in across reloads Live Browse workouts & programs Live Log meals; daily calorie & macro summary Live View your goal & its automatic daily calorie target Live Record weigh-ins; see your latest weight Live Consistency streaks Live Book & cancel trainer sessions Live Create / edit your goal in-app Beta Personal workout playlists Beta AI photo calorie estimation Planned Wearable sync (steps, heart rate) Planned

docs.title: Dive into a tab
docs.text: • Train · Eat · Book · Track progress

docs.title: Book
docs.text: Book a 1:1 session with a trainer at your branch — the whole flow is live.

docs.title: How booking works Live
docs.text:
1. Your branch is resolved automatically from your membership.
  2. Pick a trainer from the bookable trainers at that branch.
  3. Choose a day — the next 7 days are right there.
  4. Tap an available slot (shown in your local time) and confirm, with optional notes.
  5. Done — it appears under My bookings, and the slot is taken.

docs.title: My bookings Live
docs.text: See your upcoming and past sessions with their status — confirmed, completed, cancelled. Need to change plans? Cancel a confirmed booking in a tap and the slot frees up again.

docs.title: Behind the scenes
docs.text:
Trainers set their availability in the console, which generates real, bookable slots. When you book, the slot is reserved atomically so two members can't grab the same time. Cancellations and completions flow back to the gym's console.

Privacy by design

You can see the bookable trainers at your branch — not the gym's full staff roster. That's a deliberate, enforced boundary (see Roles & access).

Next: Track progress →

docs.title: Eat
docs.text: Nutrition tracking that takes seconds, tied to your goal.

docs.title: Daily summary Live
docs.text: The Eat tab shows today's calories against your target, how many you have remaining, and your macro split — protein, carbs, fat. Your target isn't a guess: it comes from your active goal.

docs.title: Log a meal Live
docs.text: Tap Log meal, pick a meal type (breakfast / lunch / dinner / snack), add a short description and calories (and macros if you like). Your summary and today's list update instantly.

docs.title: Today's meals Live
docs.text: Everything you've logged today is listed with its meal type, description and calories, so you can see where the day went at a glance.

docs.title: Streaks keep you honest Live
docs.text:
Logging consistently builds a streak — a simple, visible nudge that's one of the best predictors of sticking with it. Your current and longest streaks show on Home.

Coming next

AI photo estimation — snap a plate and get an estimated calorie/macro breakdown. It's consent-gated and on the roadmap. Planned

Next: Book →

docs.title: Track progress
docs.text: Set a goal, log the work, and watch it add up.

docs.title: Your goal drives your day Live
docs.text:
Your active goal — for example weight loss with a target weight and timeframe — becomes a daily calorie target that drives your Eat summary, so your nutrition and your goal stay in sync. The app shows your goal, its daily target, and your progress from your starting weight toward your target.

Setting or editing a goal in-app is the next surface to land Beta — the goal engine and daily-target calculation are already live in the API.

docs.title: Weigh-ins Live
docs.text: Record your weight whenever you step on the scale. Your latest weight shows in More, and your weigh-in history powers your goal progress (how far you've come from your starting weight toward your target).

docs.title: Streaks Live
docs.text: Consistency is the whole game. Logging builds a current streak and tracks your longest — small, visible wins on your Home screen that keep momentum going.

docs.title: Your account Live
docs.text:
The More tab keeps your profile, goal summary, latest weight (with quick weigh-in), backend connection status, and sign-out — all in one place. Your session persists, so you stay signed in across reloads and app restarts.

Coming next

Wearable sync — pull steps, heart rate and active calories from your watch to enrich your daily picture automatically. Planned

Back to the member overview.

docs.title: Train
docs.text: Your workouts and programs, ready to go.

docs.title: Workout library Live
docs.text:
Browse the gym's workout catalogue. Each workout shows:

  • Name and a short description
  • Duration (minutes) and difficulty (beginner → advanced)
  • Estimated calories per session
  • A video link to follow along

docs.title: Curated programs Live
docs.text: Programs bundle workouts into a plan your gym's trainers have put together — for example Beginner Kickstart or Shred & Strength. Each program shows its workouts, status, and total estimated burn, so you can pick a path instead of guessing.

docs.title: Loading, empty & error states
docs.text:
Every screen handles the real world: a spinner while loading, a friendly empty state when there's nothing yet, and a retry if the network hiccups. Pull to refresh any time.

Coming next

Personal playlists — save and sequence your own favourite workouts, with optional trainer review. The full create → submit → approve flow is already built in the API; the member screen is rolling out. Beta

Next: Eat →
